assertion_results.text: The conventions analysis identifies at least 2 patterns from sibling code: error handling with Result<T, AppError> and .context(), and the model/service/endpoints module structure
assertion_results.evidence: The conventions.md identifies both patterns explicitly under 'Discovered Conventions (from sibling analysis)': 'Error handling: All handlers and service methods return Result<T, AppError>. Errors are wrapped using .context()' and 'Module structure: Every domain module follows a strict model/ + service/ + endpoints/ tripartite structure.' Additionally identifies 10+ more conventions including endpoint handler pattern, route registration, response types, naming, testing, and database joins.
